# Brain Death

Prerequisites (all must be checked)

’ü» Coma, irreversible and cause known ’ü» Neuroimaging explains coma ’ü» CNS depressant drug effect absent (if indicated toxicology screen;  if barbiturates given, serum level < 10 ug/mL) ’ü» No evidence of residual paralytics (electrical stimulation if paralytics used) ’ü» Absence of severe acid-base, electrolyte, endocrine abnormality ’ü» Normothermia or mild hypothermia (core temp  > 36C) ’ü» Systolic BP > 100 mm Hg ’ü» No spontaneous respirations

Examination (all must be checked) ’ü» Pupils nonreactive to bright light ’ü» Corneal reflex absent ’ü» OCR absent (only test if C spine integrity ensured) ’ü» Oculovestibular reflex absent ’ü» No facial movement to noxious stimuli at supraorbital nerve, TMJ ’ü» Gag reflex absent ’ü» Cough reflex absent to tracheal suctioning ’ü» Absence of motor response to noxious stimuli in all four limbs (spinally mediated reflexes permissible)

Apnea testing (all must be checked) ’ü» Pt is hemodynamically stable ’ü» Ventilator adjusted to provide normocarbia (PaCO2 35ŌĆō45 mm Hg) ’ü» Patient preoxygenated with 100% FiO2 for >10 minutes to ’ü» PaO2 >200 mm Hg ’ü» Patient well-oxygenated with a positive end-expiratory pressure (PEEP) of 5 cm of water. ’ü» Provide oxygen via a suction catheter to the level of the carina at 6 L/min or attach T-piece with continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) at 10 cm H2O. ’ü» Disconnect ventilator ’ü» Spontaneous respirations absent ’ü» Arterial blood gas drawn at 8ŌĆō10 minutes, patient reconnected to ventilator ’ü» PCO2 Ōēź60 mm Hg, or 20 mm Hg rise from normal baseline value. OR ’ü» Apnea test aborted

Ancillary Testing To be performed only if clinical examination cannot be performed due to patient factors, or if apnea testing inconclusive or aborted. Only one needs to be performed.
- Cerebral angiogram
- HMPAO Spect
- EEG
- TCD

’ü» Document time of death (i.e. time of apnea confirmed by diagnostic ABG, or result of ancillary test if applicable)
